title: Submitting Work
description: Submit work batches to a running you-agent-factory and relate work items with DEPENDS_ON or PARENT_CHILD.
openingSummary: Submitting work is how you enqueue one or more work items into a factory that is already running, including batch documents and the relationships that order or nest those items.

whatItCovers.title: What It Covers
whatItCovers.body: This reference covers submitting work to you-agent-factory: how a FACTORY_REQUEST_BATCH is shaped, how you submit that batch to a factory that is already running, and how DEPENDS_ON and PARENT_CHILD relationships order or nest the work items in that batch. Use it when you need the web submitting-work surface rather than a full CLI flag dump or a packaged docs sync.

keyConcepts.title: Key Concepts
keyConcepts.body: Submitted work enters a factory that is already running. A batch creates multiple work items in one validated submission. Relations order or nest those items so the factory can wait on prerequisites or keep child work under a parent.

howToUse.title: How To Use
howToUse.body: Author a FACTORY_REQUEST_BATCH with a stable requestId and the works you want to enqueue. Add relations when items must wait on each other or nest under a parent. Submit the batch to a running factory, and use dry-run when you want validation without creating work. Later sections on this page teach the batch shape and relationship choices in more detail.

workBatches.title: Work Batches
workBatches.body: A FACTORY_REQUEST_BATCH is one JSON document that creates multiple work items in a single validated submission. Required fields are requestId, type set to FACTORY_REQUEST_BATCH, and a works array. Each works entry needs a name and a workTypeName that exists in the target factory. The factory validates the full batch before creating any work, so an invalid batch rejects the whole submission.

relationships.title: Relationships
relationships.body: Batch relations tell the factory how work items in the same submission should wait on each other or nest under a parent. Put them in the relations array on the FACTORY_REQUEST_BATCH. Choose DEPENDS_ON when one sibling must wait for another. Choose PARENT_CHILD when a child should belong under a parent for parent-aware fan-in. Both types can appear in the same batch when you need ordering and membership together.

limitsAndAssumptions.title: Limits And Assumptions
limitsAndAssumptions.body: This page is a web submitting-work reference for batches and relationships. It is not a full CLI flag dump, not a sync of packaged you docs, not the configuration topology page, and not the OpenAPI or API reference. Install, first-run, and command-matrix details belong on sibling documentation and guide pages.

links.submitBatchLabel: Submit that document to a factory that is already running:
links.dryRunLabel: Validate the batch without creating work:
links.idempotencyNote: Reuse a stable non-empty requestId with the same body when you retry. That makes the batch upsert idempotent instead of creating duplicate work.
links.parentChildMeaning: Source is the child of target
links.sourceTargetNote: Read DEPENDS_ON as: the source waits for the target. Read PARENT_CHILD as: the source is a child of the target. requiredState applies only to DEPENDS_ON and names the state the target must reach; it defaults to complete when omitted.
